content:
- Ramki Gaddipati Office Hours Analysis – Digital Twin Structured Summary

- II. Overall Context of the Conversation/Call

- This meeting served as a deep-dive technical and process review for the Tachyon Payments SaaS channel, primarily focusing on UPI-related functional blockers, schema/entity modeling for the Pantheon/Atlas/PNC technical stack, and the evolution of product definitions for ease of use and industry alignment. Discussion started with UPI incident handling and refund matching, including how exceptions and observations are tracked and communicated to the OPS team. The team moved on to a Pantheon–Atlas–PNC update, addressing schema propagation, entity adoption strategy (especially for Optum), and forward compatibility concerns.
- A significant portion centered on aligning terminology for product/entity definitions to ensure internal and external stakeholders have clarity, with Ramki challenging assumptions on nomenclature and data model precision. Counterpoints, clarifications, and requests for more accurate entity relationship mapping were made, pushing the team to improve precision and avoid conceptual leakage to customers. UX readiness, bug tracking, and the schedule for feature rollouts—leading into April 2025—rounded out the call.

- IV. Key Takeaways from the Call

- Clarity Required on UPI Refund Matching Logic: Team aligned on using a new approach, leveraging reference ID and VPA for unmatched refunds (“relied on… ref ID and VPA…”).
- Exceptions vs. Observations Must Flow to OPS: Unmatched cases to be sent as observations for OPS review, rather than only exceptions (“go in as an observation, if not as an exception…”).
- Mandate Support & Exception Handling Broadened: Mandate and failed push provisioning should surface both as exceptions and as observations, ensuring OPS visibility (“may have a queuing… as an observation”).
- Pantheon–Atlas–PNC Entity Propagation Schedule Finalized: Multiple propagation/development targets committed for March, especially for Optum onboarding (“all the entities… propagated to… respective cluster…”).
- Optum Prioritization Clarified: Prioritize onboarding of all Pantheon/PNC entities required by Optum for a “fresh start”, with grandfathered entities handled later (“you can prioritize the first setup…”).
- Precision in Product Hierarchy Urged: Ramki rejected terminology sprawl—insisting on distinct “issuance” and “acceptance” product terms, and alignment on what constitutes a “product”, “provider”, or “relationship”.
- UX Design to Be Finalized by March End: Team agreed on delivering final UX mocks and entity modeling by end of March, with development starting April (“UX… completing in March”).
- Open Points on Forward Compatibility: Pending scope to be finalized for cross-system visibility and modification rights; concurrent writes to be specially managed (“forward compatibility… can follow…”).

- V. Ramki's Insights

1. Emphasis on Rigorous Exception/Observation Process: Ramki underscored the need for a clear, actionable observation flow to the OPS team, not just classic exceptions, expressing the operational importance and expectation for review even in ambiguous/unmatched credit scenarios.

2. Demand for Terminological and Structural Precision: He challenged the use of overlapping terminology (e.g., “payment user product” vs. “issuance product”), pressing for clarity and practical distinction, especially separating how internal models manifest towards bank users. Ramki highlighted the pitfalls of ambiguous or leaky abstractions and insisted on discrete definitions for products, providers, and relationships, showing skepticism towards loose product hierarchies or overloading terms.

3. Mandate on Not Exposing Resource Product Internally: Ramki reiterated that certain internal constructs (e.g., “resource product”) must not surface to external or bank personas. He was clear that terminology tailored for platforms must be shielded from customer-facing interfaces to avoid confusion.

4. Approach to Prioritization & Grandfathering: He recommended aggressive prioritization for new setups (Optum) to onboard essential entities in Pantheon/PNC from day one, while allowing for separate handling (“grandfathering”) of legacy or non-critical entities.

5. Demand for Actionable UX and Data Model Artifacts: Ramki was unwilling to approve mental/conceptual models in the absence of detailed, precise entity diagrams, particularly flagging misunderstandings or incomplete data-model associations (e.g., SKUs, card product to payment product relationships).

6. Preparedness for April Development: Ramki confirmed agreement with the schedule for UX sign-off by March end and anticipated seeing actual product modeling and development for Optum from April, signaling expectation for tangible progress.

- VI. Ramki's Suggested Action Items

Action Item 1
- Task: Update the wording and logic for UPI refund matching, ensuring unmatched transactions are clarified and exceptions/observations are sent to the OPS team.
- Assigned To: Gaurav Doshi and Akash Pal
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Revise documentation to explicitly distinguish between matched and unmatched logic and ensure any unmatchable items surface as observations to OPS, enabling an operational review process and possible automation.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate/Current Quarter

Action Item 2
- Task: Prioritize implementation of Pantheon/PNC entities required for Optum onboarding, focusing only on critical sets, with grandfathered entities sequenced afterward.
- Assigned To: Jim Kim's team (general)
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Focus initial adoption and propagation on entities that Optum needs from day one. Ensure fresh setups rely strictly on Pantheon entities, deferring complex forward-compatibility/grandfathering concerns to a later phase.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: By end of March for solutioning; development to start in April.

Action Item 3
- Task: Refine entity relationship diagrams and product hierarchy nomenclature, distilling to unambiguous terms—especially separating “issuance” from “acceptance” products, and clearly modeling provider–relationship–product linkages.
- Assigned To: Jim Kim, Abhilash Rao, UX/Data Modeling team
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Present complete, precise entity diagrams, avoiding leaky abstractions; ensure no confusing or duplicate terminology is presented in customer-facing flows.
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Prior to UX sign-off (EOM March)

Action Item 4
- Task: Provide a consolidated, current list of priority bugs (with screen grab or filtered link), segmented by source (engineering, CS, etc.), for Ramki’s review.
- Assigned To: Akash Pal
- Ramki's Instructions on Layout/Methodology: Share via chat or any expedient means, but ensure clarity about pending ownership for each open critical bug (especially P1 & P2).
- Timeline Mentioned by Ramki: Immediate

- VII. Jira Ticket

- Scope: Update UPI refund matching and observation process flow as per clarified logic and escalate as observations/exceptions to OPS
- Assignee: Gaurav Doshi / Akash Pal
- Justification: Affects operational reliability; clear gap in how unmatched cases are handled and surfaced to OPS. Immediate change required for production quality and compliance.

- Scope: Refine and document Pantheon–Atlas–PNC entity adoption and propagation for Optum onboarding. Focus strictly on MVP entities, producing entity diagrams and structures for UX sign-off.
- Assignee: Jim Kim’s team, with UX/data modeling support
- Justification: High priority for upcoming Optum cutover; ensures forward progress on feature go-live and internal alignment.

- Scope: (No Jira ticket for bug list consolidation as this is a hygiene activity already tracked in existing operational pipelines.)

- VIII. Follow Up Questions/Suggestions

- Request a working session (whiteboard or screen-share) to refine the entity model and directly resolve product/provider/relationship ambiguities, as verbal abstractions are leading to continued misunderstandings.
- Ensure that the OPS team is briefed on the revised observation workflow, with clear SOPs and automation guidelines where feasible.
- Recommend a pre-April review checkpoint for the finalized UX mock-ups to ensure all of Ramki’s terminology, configuration, and product hierarchy concerns have been addressed before development starts.
- Suggest partnership between data modeling and UX teams to co-own the process of translating backend structures into unambiguous user-facing interfaces and documentation, minimizing future leakage of technical constructs.

entities: UPI refund matching, inward credits, unmatched transaction logic, exception vs. observation handling, mandate support, schema propagation, Pantheon adoption, PNC entity onboarding, forward compatibility, payment product definitions, UX alignment, terminology clarification, Optum prioritization, bug tracking, product–provider–relationship modeling, refactoring

title: 2025-03-07_Tachyon Payments SaaS  Check-in (7)
